entities.name: Product-Market Fit
entities.context: Measure of market pull, not technical robustness; can be validated through manual execution before building software

entities.name: Manual-to-Product Debt
entities.context: Operational debt incurred when selling manual methodology as finished software; business becomes dependent on expensive human intervention

entities.name: Selling the Product Before It Exists
entities.context: High-leverage founder strategy of using humans to execute backend manually while presenting as automated product

entities.name: Living Prototypes
entities.context: Treating humans executing manual backend as prototypes for eventual system requirements

relationships.from: Selling the Product Before It Exists
relationships.context: Strategy enables rapid iteration on solution logic to find PMF, bypassing slow development cycles

relationships.from: Selling the Product Before It Exists
relationships.context: Strategy creates this unique form of operational debt requiring high-risk transition
The humans creating the debt also serve as living prototypes capturing nuances for automation
